title: INSERT Statement
content:
### INSERT Statement

INSERT adds new rows of data to a table. It is one of the four fundamental DML operations (INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE).

---

### Basic INSERT Syntax

```sql
INSERT INTO employees (id, name, department, salary)
VALUES (1, 'Alice', 'Engineering', 75000);
```

### INSERT Multiple Rows

```sql
INSERT INTO employees (name, department, salary)
VALUES
    ('Bob', 'Sales', 65000),
    ('Charlie', 'Marketing', 70000),
    ('Diana', 'Engineering', 80000);
```

### INSERT with Default Values

```sql
INSERT INTO employees (id, name)
VALUES (5, 'Eve');
-- Other columns use their DEFAULT values or NULL
```

### INSERT INTO SELECT

```sql
-- Copy data from one table to another
INSERT INTO archived_employees (id, name, salary)
SELECT id, name, salary
FROM employees
WHERE hire_date < '2020-01-01';
```

### INSERT IGNORE / ON DUPLICATE KEY

```sql
-- MySQL: Skip if duplicate key
INSERT IGNORE INTO users (email, name)
VALUES ('alice@example.com', 'Alice');

-- MySQL: Update if duplicate key
INSERT INTO users (id, name)
VALUES (1, 'Alice Updated')
ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE name = VALUES(name);

-- PostgreSQL: Upsert
INSERT INTO users (id, name)
VALUES (1, 'Alice')
ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET name = EXCLUDED.name;
```

> **Key Point:** Always list the columns explicitly in INSERT statements for clarity and to prevent errors when table schemas change.
codeExample:
INSERT INTO products (name, sku, price, category)
VALUES
    ('Wireless Mouse', 'WM-001', 29.99, 'Electronics'),
    ('USB-C Hub', 'USB-001', 49.99, 'Electronics');

quiz.question: Can INSERT add multiple rows in a single statement?
options: No, one row at a time
Yes, by providing multiple value tuples

quiz.question: What does INSERT INTO SELECT do?
options: Inserts rows from a query result into an existing table
Creates a new table from a query
